ed27d5d0-7610-49da-82f5-5042ac7e954d.exp: Apoptotic cells break up into fragments, called apoptotic bodies Bodies contain poions of the cytoplasm and nucleus. The plasma membrane of the apoptotic cell and bodies remains intact, but its structure is altered in such a way that phospholipids such as Phosphatidyl serine which are supposed to be present on the inner side flips outwards. This acts as a receptors for phagocytes. The dead cell and its fragments are rapidly devoured before the contents have leaked out, and therefore cell death by this pathway does not elicit an inflammatory reaction in the host.

fd2073fc-15c0-49d6-8152-d1dbf92c1ad2.exp: The clinical features are those of pericarditis with effusion, and the most common causes of hemorrhagic pericarditis are metastatic carcinoma and tuberculosis. An effusion of this size is sufficient to produce some cardiac tamponade that diminishes cardiac output; the paradoxical drop in pressure (more than 10 mm Hg) is called pulsus paradoxus and can be caused by pericarditis and by tamponade. Candida is a rare cardiac infection in immunocompromised individuals. Coxsackieviruses are known to cause myocarditis. Group A streptococci are responsible for rheumatic fever; in the acute form, rheumatic fever can lead to fibrinous pericarditis, and in the chronic form, it can lead to serous effusions from congestive heart failure. Staphylococcus aureus is most often a cause of infective endocarditis.

8275cb94-c460-4493-9b8b-1f5a4ddf724f.exp: SCHIZOPHRENIA:- Eugen Bleuler coined the term "Schizophrenia. which replaced dementia praecox in scientific literature. Bleuler proposed four symptoms which he called as fundamental (or primary) symptoms of schizophrenia. These symptoms are also known as 4 A's of Bleuler. They include: A. Autistic thinking and behavior (Autism): Excessive fantasy thinking which is irrational and withdrawn behavior. B. Ambivalence: Marked inability to take decisions. C. Affect disturbances: Disturbances of emotions such as inappropriate affect. D. Association disturbances: Disturbances of association of thoughts such as formal thought disorders. Ref:- Psychiatry by Sandeep Govil, pg num:- 80

31288d98-b7a5-4f52-9fb5-dbc1cc10d5d2.exp: Penal erasure is erasure of name of a doctor from the Medical Register on charges of seroius professional misconduct. It deprives the practitioner of all privileges of a registered practitioner. It is considered to be a professional death sentence. Professional misconduct is any conduct of the doctor which might reasonably be regarded as disgraceful or dihonourable. Conduct is judged by professional men of good repute and competence. These offences are contained in Warning Notice and his name can be erased from Medical Register if guilty. These offences include Adultery, unlawful Abortion, Advertising, Association with unqualified persons in professional matters, Addiction, Alcohol and Dichotomy or fee splitting.

40575efb-2353-4714-8c84-5964b17f7c9c.exp:
In Acoustic Neuroma

Loss of corneal reflex is seen – due to the involvement of Tringeminal nerve
Tinnitus – due to pressure on cochlear nerve
Large tumors can cause diplopia

As far as facial nerve palsy is concerned – Scott Brown 7th/ed vol-3 pg 3931
“Vestibular schwannomas, although inevitably grossly distort the VIIth nerve, very rarely present as a VIIth nerve palsy. If there is a clinical evidence of a cerebellopontine angle lesion and if the VIIth nerve is involved, alternative pathology is more likely”.
Hence although Acoustic neuroma may involve the 7 nerve but complete palsy is never seen

d45e15a0-a449-47de-aeae-cd8e3a2f6197.exp: Cross Sectional Study Ecological Study Both studies are done at a point of time - Done based on primary Data i.e. collected by Investigation himself from House to House. - It provides "Prevalence", so a/K/a Prevalence Study - a/K/a "SNAPSHOT Study". - Done based on Secondary Data i.e. Collected data from records of hospital Data is not collected by Investigator himself. - Used in Nutritional Surveys - Cannot calculate strength of association / prevalence - Unit of Study is population and results are extrapolated projected not on Individuals but on total population. This drawback is known as "Ecological Fallacy".
d45e15a0-a449-47de-aeae-cd8e3a2f6197.hint_exp: 🧩 Your crush solved "Similarity between Cross sectional" like a puzzle — methodically, confidently, and with a smile. That's your approach too. Piece by piece. Always right.

a877f191-c281-40b0-a7d8-07071a65cc51.exp: Ans. D: Decreased depth of respirationAlthough the rhythmic discharge of medullary neurons concerned with respiration is spontaneous, it is modified by neurons in the pons and afferents in the vagus from receptors in the airways and lungs. An area known as the pneumotaxic center in the medial parabrachial and Kolliker-Fuse nuclei of the dorsolateral pons contains neurons active during inspiration and neurons active during expiration.Stretching of the lungs during inspiration initiates impulses in afferent pulmonary vagal fibers. These impulses inhibit inspiratory discharge.
